knowledge_gaps: Sanctions architecture has permanently altered the global equity investable universe with ~$3-5T removed from indices and $40-60B annual compliance costs
EM equity has structurally bifurcated along geopolitical alignment lines with 25-40% cumulative outperformance of aligned vs non-aligned EMs since 2022

Buy the dip is a regime-specific strategy whose post-2009 success reflects ZIRP/QE/fiscal support, not a structural tendency — pre-2009/cross-country base rate for >20% drawdown recovery is ~52-60 months
Small cap vs large cap divergence drivers
Central bank independence erosion would increase ERP by 50-80bp per standard deviation decline in independence, with Fed effective independence at lowest since pre-Volcker era
AI capex cycle sustainability and rotation timing
Energy sector weight at ~3.5-4.0% of S&P 500 despite 8-10% FCF yields signals structural mispricing of hydrocarbon demand decline
US equity exceptionalism is survivorship bias — the 6.4% annualized real return is an outlier in a 35-country sample where the median is ~3-4%
ROIC-WACC spread compression to 3.5-5.0pp creates convex rate sensitivity — current S&P 500 is ~2.5x more fragile to rate shocks than 2017-19
focus.instruction: This topic has been studied at overview level. Focus on: (1) specific subtopics listed, (2) closing identified knowledge gaps, (3) cross-topic interactions with connected topics, (4) edge cases and regime-dependent behavior.
